items.title: Enormous ship en route to pick up world's largest electric ferry

children.content: The 217-metre-long heavy-lift vessel Black Marlin will deliver a Tasmanian shipbuilder's new electric ferry to South America, after the previous transporter became caught up in the Strait of Hormuz closure.

gallery.altText: A gigantic ship in the middle of the ocean

thumbnail.altText: A gigantic ship in the middle of the ocean
A large electric ferry named China Zorrilla on the River Derwent in Hobart.

items.title: Damning report questions what counts as a home in Tasmania's 10,000-home target

children.content: A financial review into Tasmania's soon-to-be-axed state housing authority says it relies too heavily on spreadsheets, and noted problems with transparency and effective decision-making.

image.altText: A shot of several half-built homes.

items.title: Premier tells member to take complaint to body which doesn't exist yet

children.content: Jeremy Rockliff won't investigate how an email containing the health-related information of a politician was leaked to the media and suggests she could take up the matter with a complaints body that doesn't exist yet.

image.altText: Man looks out to the distance, leaning on a green chair

items.title: As cost of living bites, one of the things slipping may be fitness goals

children.content: As household budgets tighten, keeping up a health and fitness regime often slips, but experts say giving people cheap or free ways to stay fit is critical to longer-term wellbeing.

gallery.altText: People working out in a community hall

thumbnail.altText: People working out in a community hall

items.title: Search continues for Tasmanian devil 'Mary' missing from Gold Coast wildlife park

children.content: The recently arrived young devil has been kept in a secure indoor facility while quarantining, but was noticed missing yesterday morning.

image.altText: Tasmanian Devil joeys walks through grass

items.title: Ban on nurses wearing colourful scrubs at Hobart hospital to be overturned

children.content: Nurses at the Royal Hobart Hospital have been told they should stop wearing fun scrubs or "free dress" and reminded they need to maintain "some professionalism" regarding their uniform.

image.altText: Woman in hospital scrubs opening a filing cabinet.

items.title: 'Terribly sad day': Beer experts say Boag's closure points to growing cost of brewing

children.content: Beer industry stakeholders say it's a "sad day for the history of Tassie beer" after the announcement Boag's brewery in Launceston will close.

articles.title: James Boag ends 145-year history as brewer ceases Tasmanian production

children.content: The famed northern Tasmanian brewer announces it will cease production in the state by November and repay a government loan it secured to keep its Launceston brewery visitor centre open.

image.altText: James Boags Beers 2024-06-25 10:06:00

items.title: Trump drops $2.5b 'weaponization' fund plans amid corruption allegations

children.content: The US president has abandoned plans for a $2.51 billion "anti-weaponization" fund for ‌victims of alleged government "weaponization", according to the acting attorney-general.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaking during a cabinet meeting at the White House.

items.title: Trump names inexperienced ally as intelligence director

children.content: Bill Pulte, who is known for publicly attacking the US president's political enemies, would also retain his existing jobs overseeing federal housing and mortgage policies.

image.altText: A man in a suit smiles.

items.title: Marles says Australia wanted three second-hand subs all along

children.content: The defence minister says the new AUKUS deal that will see Australia buy three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the US is what the government had wanted all along. Follow live.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks during an AUKUS Defence ministers' press huddle

items.title: UK PM calls video of handcuffed dying teen 'harrowing' amid protests

children.content: The British prime minister says he "felt sick" while watching a video released by police of 18-year-old Henry Nowak, who was handcuffed while he lay dying from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack.

gallery.altText: A crowd of mostly white men at a protest under cardboard 'Henry Nowak' and 'All Lives Matter' signs.
A man and woman holding up a poster of bloody handcuffs and a picture of a Caucasian boy in a white shirt and blue tie.
A man wearing a UK flag with his arm raised, in front of a line of police in protective helmets behind riot shields.

thumbnail.altText: A crowd of mostly white men at a protest under cardboard 'Henry Nowak' and 'All Lives Matter' signs.
A man and woman holding up a poster of bloody handcuffs and a picture of a Caucasian boy in a white shirt and blue tie.
A man wearing a UK flag with his arm raised, in front of a line of police in protective helmets behind riot shields.

items.title: Rocket motor test success marks Australian-made missile milestone

children.content: In what is being described as a significant leap forward in Australia's push to develop and manufacture its own missiles, newly released footage shows the first test-firing of a locally made solid rocket motor.

image.altText: A trail of fire appearing out of a rocket motor as it is shot.

items.title: Plenty of jobs but no housing: Could 'flat-pack' homes help save this country town?

children.content: A Queensland outback town pins its hopes of solving a local housing crisis on "flat-pack" homes as the high cost of building risks paralysing the community.

gallery.altText: white house without a roof sits on block of land in the outback
woman with short blonde hair stand on the veranda of a house

thumbnail.altText: white house without a roof sits on block of land in the outback
woman with short blonde hair stand on the veranda of a house

items.title: Heat-related deaths 'tip of the iceberg' in India as UN sends El Niño warning

children.content: India's deadly heatwave comes as the UN warns the world to prepare for a moderate to strong El Niño this year.

image.altText: A man puts his head under a running outdoor tap.

items.title: Semaglutide is used for weight loss but may benefit kidneys and heart

children.content: Research shows semaglutide — commonly sold as Ozempic or Wegovy — can improve kidney function and survival rates for people with some chronic diseases, even if they have heart issues.

image.altText: Two boxes of medication side by side, labelled Ozempic and Wegovy

items.title: Why Hugh Jackman's brother cried on his first day as a prison-school teacher

children.content: After Ralph Jackman retrained as a teacher, his first job was in a youth detention centre. His new memoir reveals how a broken system is failing vulnerable kids.

gallery.altText: Three teenage boys sitting at desks in a classroom, facing the teacher, who is standing in front of a whiteboard.
A balding man with cropped hair, wearing a striped button-down shirt.

thumbnail.altText: Three teenage boys sitting at desks in a classroom, facing the teacher, who is standing in front of a whiteboard.
A balding man with cropped hair, wearing a striped button-down shirt.

items.title: This rapper was having panic attacks on stage, until he transitioned

children.content: Poet, rapper, musician, playwright and novelist Kae Tempest is a prodigious polymath. It wasn't until he affirmed his gender that he connected with his true self.

image.altText: Close-up of Kae Tempest looking down at sunset, with a city skyline in silhouette behind them.

items.title: Quiz: Hold on to your hat, we're off on a tour of the globe

children.content: The famous US road trip along Route 66 links which two cities? And which country eats the most garlic per person?

image.altText: A statue of a woman with a city in the background and a full moon above her head

items.title: Major Russian attack on Ukraine leaves at least 22 dead and dozens injured

children.content: Moscow targeted Kyiv and other cities, killing multiple people, damaging buildings and leaving wounded trapped under rubble, local authorities say.

image.altText: Bright flash and smoke over Kyiv skyline at night.

gallery.altText: The silhouette of a man taking a photo on an empty road underneath a fiery red skyline clouded by black smoke.

thumbnail.altText: The silhouette of a man taking a photo on an empty road underneath a fiery red skyline clouded by black smoke.

items.title: ASX trades higher as AI drives Wall Street to record highs, oil rises on US-Iran uncertainty

children.content: Oil prices jump to a one-week high as the US and Iran provide conflicting updates on their peace talks. The Australian share market starts its day slightly higher after a surge in AI stocks pushed Wall Street to fresh record highs. Follow live.

image.altText: Giant screen on the wall of the ASX, with large green numbers, showing the price of shares which have risen.

items.title: Former Canberra Liberals leader leaves party citing 'threat of physical assault'

children.content: Former Canberra Liberals leader Leanne Castley says she has experienced "bullying, intimidation, lies and a threat of physical assault" in the past 18 months.

image.altText: A politician in a pink jacket stands in front of microphones.

items.title: Emotional Ukrainian star not accepting silence from Russian players

children.content: Ukrainian star Marta Kostyuk says it is unacceptable for Russians to stay silent on the war, especially after Daria Kasatkina's defection to Australia.

image.altText: Marta Kostyuk wipes away a tear during an on-court interview at the French Open.

items.title: Poor advertising, confused voters and underfunded candidates: SA Voice's problems revealed

children.content: New documents suggest South Australia's inaugural Voice to Parliament election was hamstrung by advertising failures that left First Nations voters confused or unaware an election was taking place.

gallery.altText: Scott Wilson looks at the camera with a serious look on his face. He has grey hair, a full beard, and is wearing a black jacket.

thumbnail.altText: Scott Wilson looks at the camera with a serious look on his face. He has grey hair, a full beard, and is wearing a black jacket.

items.title: Cancer patient's stem cell transplant struggle highlights need for more donors

children.content: Sarah Taylor is one of approximately 1,200 Australians urgently waiting for a life-saving stem cell transplant. Advocates say Australia's registry is "ridiculously small" compared to other nations, leading to potentially fatal consequences.

image.altText: A young woman cuddles a teddy bear as she sits in a hospital bed.

items.title: Green top-scores and Ellis stands tall as Australia levels ODI series

children.content: Nathan Ellis shines with the ball, while Cameron Green tops the run-scoring for Australia in a 41-run ODI victory over Pakistan in Lahore.

image.altText: Cameron Green cuts a cricket ball away as Pakistan's wicketkeeper looks on.

items.title: Dieback disease killing mango trees is spreading in the NT

children.content: At a field day in the Northern Territory, mango growers were desperate to learn what could be done to stop the spread of mango twig tip dieback.

image.altText: Needles injected into mango trees

items.title: Animals displaced, residents say, as controversial sports project green lit

children.content: A sports development in Sydney's north-west has been accused by residents of killing and displacing wildlife.

image.altText: A wallaby stands behind a tree with its face visible with silver fencing seen in the foreground.

items.title: Plan for Stars and Renegades to merge in BBL shake-up

children.content: Cricket Victoria plans to merge the Stars and Renegades into one "Melbourne" team.

gallery.altText: a batter dressed in green hits the ball, while a keeper in red is behind him at the stumps

thumbnail.altText: a batter dressed in green hits the ball, while a keeper in red is behind him at the stumps

items.title: Government says it preferred second-hand submarines

children.content: Australia and the United States' initial positions about delivering a new submarine under AUKUS differed, Senate estimates has revealed.

image.altText: Part of the hull and control room of a submarine that has surfaced from underwater.

items.title: Unfinished outback opal museum gets $1m lifeline to pay debts

children.content: Construction of the museum boasting the world's greatest public collection of opalised dinosaur fossils has been on hold since the project ran out of money last year.

gallery.altText: An artistic imaging of a modernistic building surrounded by trees

thumbnail.altText: An artistic imaging of a modernistic building surrounded by trees

avatar.alt: Catherine James looks at the camera.
